headline: OpenAI Closes Record $122 Billion Funding Round at $852 Billion Valuation
raw_snippet: OpenAI closed its latest funding round with $122 billion in committed capital at a post-money valuation of $852 billion, the largest private funding round in history. SoftBank co-led the round alongside a16z, D.E. Shaw Ventures, MGX, TPG, and T. Rowe Price, with strategic participation from Amazon ($50B), NVIDIA ($30B), SoftBank ($30B), and Microsoft. The round grew from the $110 billion announced in February after additional commitments.

headline: OpenAI Opens Equity to Retail Investors for First Time, Raises $3 Billion from Individuals
raw_snippet: For the first time, OpenAI extended participation to individual investors through bank channels, raising $3 billion from retail participants as part of the $122 billion round. OpenAI will also be included in several ARK Invest exchange-traded funds, further broadening ownership. The move is widely interpreted as groundwork for a widely anticipated IPO that could land as early as Q4 2026.

headline: Amazon Invests $50 Billion in OpenAI, with $35 Billion Contingent on IPO or AGI Milestone
raw_snippet: Amazon agreed to invest $50 billion in OpenAI's funding round in two stages: $15 billion in Series C Preferred Stock due March 31, and a $35 billion commitment contingent on OpenAI going public or reaching the milestone of artificial general intelligence. Separately, OpenAI expanded its existing $38 billion AWS agreement by $100 billion over eight years, committing to consume 2 gigawatts of Trainium capacity through AWS.

headline: ARK Invest Adds OpenAI to Three Flagship ETFs at Roughly 3% Allocation
raw_snippet: Cathie Wood's ARK Invest added OpenAI to three flagship ETFs — ARKK, ARKW, and ARKF — each allocating roughly 3% to the private AI company via Series C private placement. The three funds collectively acquired 348,995 OpenAI shares or units. The inclusion gives retail investors indirect exposure to pre-IPO OpenAI equity through publicly traded exchange-traded funds.

headline: OpenAI Reports $2 Billion Monthly Revenue and 900 Million Weekly Active ChatGPT Users
raw_snippet: Alongside its funding round close, OpenAI disclosed it is generating $2 billion in monthly revenue ($13.1 billion in 2025) with more than 900 million weekly active ChatGPT users including over 50 million subscribers. Enterprise now accounts for over 40% of revenue and is on track to reach parity with consumer by end of 2026. Codex serves over 2 million weekly users, up 5x in three months.

headline: CFO Sarah Friar Warns 2026 IPO Timeline Too Aggressive, Reportedly at Odds with Altman
raw_snippet: OpenAI CFO Sarah Friar has internally flagged the company's Q4 2026 IPO timeline as too aggressive, putting her at odds with CEO Sam Altman. Friar's concerns center on over $600 billion in cloud server capacity commitments over five years and uncertainty about whether slowing revenue growth will support them. Altman has reportedly excluded Friar from some investor conversations, and Friar stopped reporting to Altman, instead reporting to Fidji Simo.

headline: OpenAI Acquires TBPN Tech Talk Show in First Media Company Acquisition
raw_snippet: OpenAI acquired TBPN (Technology Business Programming Network), a daily podcast hosted by John Coogan and Jordi Hays covering tech, business, and AI. TBPN is on track to earn over $30 million in 2026 revenue. The show will report to OpenAI's chief political operative Chris Lehane but retain editorial independence. Deal terms were not disclosed. This is OpenAI's first acquisition of a media company.

headline: ChatGPT Launches Voice Mode in Apple CarPlay for Hands-Free Conversations
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Voice mode in Apple CarPlay, allowing users with iOS 26.4 or later to start voice conversations with ChatGPT directly from their car dashboard. The experience is strictly voice-first with no text or visual responses on screen. ChatGPT cannot control car functions or access maps, live location, or vehicle data — those tasks still require Siri. Users can also resume existing mobile conversations.

headline: OpenAI Launches Visual Product Discovery in ChatGPT via Expanded Agentic Commerce Protocol
raw_snippet: OpenAI launched richer visual product discovery in ChatGPT through its expanded Agentic Commerce Protocol (ACP). Users can browse products visually, compare options side-by-side with pricing and reviews, and upload images to find similar items. Retailers including Target, Sephora, Nordstrom, Lowe's, Best Buy, The Home Depot, and Wayfair have integrated into ACP. The feature is rolling out to all ChatGPT tiers including Free.

headline: Codex Launches Pay-As-You-Go Pricing for Business and Enterprise Teams
raw_snippet: OpenAI introduced Codex-only seats with pay-as-you-go pricing for ChatGPT Business and Enterprise teams, removing the need for a fixed seat fee. Usage is billed on token consumption with no rate limits. Eligible Business workspaces receive $100 in promotional credits per new Codex team member (up to $500 per team). Codex now serves over 2 million weekly users with usage growing 70%+ month-over-month.

headline: OpenAI Cuts ChatGPT Business Annual Price from $25 to $20 Per Seat
raw_snippet: OpenAI lowered the annual price of ChatGPT Business from $25 to $20 per seat, a 20% reduction. Existing subscribers on both monthly and annual plans will receive a credit applied to their next subscription renewal. The price cut was announced alongside new Codex pay-as-you-go pricing, as OpenAI pushes to accelerate enterprise adoption ahead of a potential IPO.

headline: GPT-4o Fully Retired from Custom GPTs Across All ChatGPT Plans
raw_snippet: OpenAI completed the final phase of GPT-4o's retirement from ChatGPT on April 3, 2026. Business, Enterprise, and Edu customers who had retained GPT-4o access within Custom GPTs lost it, with GPTs auto-migrating to GPT-5.3 Instant or GPT-5.4 Thinking equivalents. GPT-4o was initially retired from consumer plans on February 13 but Business/Enterprise received an extended grace period. GPT-4o remains available in the API with no announced retirement.

headline: Fidji Simo Takes Medical Leave for Neuroimmune Condition, Triggering Major Leadership Reshuffle
raw_snippet: Fidji Simo, OpenAI's head of AGI deployment and applications, announced several weeks of medical leave to address a relapse of postural orthostatic tachycardia syndrome (POTS). In an internal memo, Simo said she had 'postponed medical tests and new therapies to stay completely focused on the job' but 'pushed a little too far.' President Greg Brockman will oversee product in her absence. The announcement triggered a broader executive reshuffle.

headline: COO Brad Lightcap Shifts to Special Projects Role; Denise Dresser Takes Over Commercial Operations
raw_snippet: OpenAI's longtime COO Brad Lightcap transitioned to leading 'special projects' focused on complex deals and investments, reporting directly to CEO Sam Altman. CRO Denise Dresser, the former Slack CEO who recently joined OpenAI, will take over most of Lightcap's commercial duties. CSO Jason Kwon, CFO Sarah Friar, and Dresser will split oversight of business and operations. The moves come as OpenAI prepares for a potential 2026 IPO.

headline: CMO Kate Rouch Steps Down for Cancer Recovery; Former Meta CMO Gary Briggs Named Interim
raw_snippet: Kate Rouch, OpenAI's chief marketing officer, stepped down from her role to focus on late-stage breast cancer recovery. She was diagnosed roughly a year and a half ago, shortly after joining OpenAI. Former Meta CMO Gary Briggs is stepping in on an interim basis. Rouch is expected to return in a 'different, more narrowly scoped role when her health allows.' She is the third executive affected in one week alongside Simo and Lightcap.

headline: OpenAI Publishes 'Inside Our Approach to the Model Spec' Detailing AI Behavior Framework
raw_snippet: OpenAI published a detailed blog post outlining the philosophy and mechanics behind its Model Spec, a 100-page document that defines how OpenAI's models adjudicate between obligations to society, OpenAI, developers, and end users. The Model Spec is designed as a public interface so researchers, policymakers, and the public can 'read, inspect, and debate' intended model behavior. OpenAI also updated the spec to strengthen principles for teen user experiences.

headline: Federal Judge Orders OpenAI to Release 20 Million Anonymized ChatGPT Logs in Copyright Case
raw_snippet: A federal judge affirmed an order requiring OpenAI to turn over 20 million anonymized ChatGPT conversation logs to plaintiffs in the consolidated AI copyright lawsuit. OpenAI had argued the disclosure would compromise user privacy, but the judge sided with the magistrate judge's earlier ruling. The logs are sought by publishers and authors who allege OpenAI trained its models on copyrighted material without permission.
